THE COURT FINDS THAT A COMMUNITY CONTROL SANCTION WILL ADEQUATELY PROTECT THE PUBLIC AND WILL NOT DEMEAN THE SERIOUSNESS OF THE OFFENSE. IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED THAT THE DEFENDANT IS SENTENCED TO TWO YEARS OF COMMUNITY CONTROL ON EACH COUNT, UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF THE ADULT PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITH THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1.) DEFENDANT TO ABIDE BY ALL RULES AND REGULATIONS OF THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT. 2.) DEFENDANT TO BE SUPERVISED BY: GROUP D 3.) REPORT WEEKLY FOR THREE MONTHS AND EVERY TWO WEEKS THEREAFTER OR AS DIRECTED BY PO. 4.) ATTEND PROGRAMMING AS INDICATED IN CASE PLAN. 5.) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PAY A MONTHLY SUPERVISION FEE OF $20.00. 6.) DEFENDANT IS ELIGIBLE FOR EARLY TERMINATION REQUEST WHEN ALL CONDITIONS HAVE BEEN MET. 7.) RANDOM DRUG TESTING. 8.) CONDITIONS AND TERMS OF PROBATION ARE SUBJECT TO MODIFICATION BY THE PROBATION OFFICER AND APPROVAL OF THE COURT. VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS MAY RESULT IN EXTENSION OF THE PERIOD OF SUPERVISION, MORE RESTRICTIVE SANCTIONS, OR A PRISON TERM OF 12 MONTHS IN COUNTS 2, 3, 4 AS APPROVED BY LAW. THE DEFENDANT IS REFERRED TO THE COUNTY PROBATION DEPARTMENT FOR A POST-SENTENCE INVESTIGATION AND REPORT. AS A RESULT OF THE CONVICTION(S) IN THIS CASE AND THE IMPOSITION OF A PRISON SENTENCE, AND PURSUANT TO R.C. 2967.28(F)(4)(C), THE DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBJECT TO A PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL OF: UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. THE ADULT PAROLE AUTHORITY WILL ADMINISTER THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL PURSUANT TO R.C.2967.28, AND THE DEFENDANT HAS BEEN ADVISED THAT IF THE DEFENDANT VIOLATES POST-RELEASE CONTROL, THE PAROLE BOARD MAY IMPOSE A PRISON TERM AS PART OF THE SENTENCE OF UP TO HALF OF THE STATED PRISON TERM OR STATED MINIMUM TERM ORIGINALLY IMPOSED UPON THE DEFENDANT IN NINE-MONTH INCREMENTS. IF WHILE ON POST-RELEASE CONTROL THE DEFENDANT IS CONVICTED OF A NEW FELONY, THE SENTENCING COURT WILL HAVE AUTHORITY TO TERMINATE THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL AND ORDER A CONSECUTIVE PRISON TERM OF UP TO THE GREATER OF TWELVE MONTHS OR THE REMAINING PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL. nightmare_events: 04/03/2025 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. FAILURE OF THE COMPLAINING WITNESS TO APPEAR, WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, WILL RESULT IN DISMISSAL OF THE CASE FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE. 04/03/2025 CPDL2 04/03/2025 13:23:32

ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. DEFENDANT ADDRESSES THE COURT, PROSECUTOR GRANT MORGAN ADDRESSES THE COURT. DEFENDANT ADVISED OF POST RELEASE CONTROL FOR UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. THE COURT CONSIDERED ALL REQUIRED FACTORS OF THE LAW. THE COURT FINDS THAT A COMMUNITY CONTROL SANCTION WILL ADEQUATELY PROTECT THE PUBLIC AND WILL NOT DEMEAN THE SERIOUSNESS OF THE OFFENSE. IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED THAT THE DEFENDANT IS SENTENCED TO TWO YEARS OF COMMUNITY CONTROL ON EACH COUNT, UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF THE ADULT PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITH THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1.) DEFENDANT TO ABIDE BY ALL RULES AND REGULATIONS OF THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT. 2.) DEFENDANT TO BE SUPERVISED BY: GROUP D 3.) REPORT WEEKLY FOR THREE MONTHS AND EVERY TWO WEEKS THEREAFTER OR AS DIRECTED BY PO. 4.) ATTEND PROGRAMMING AS INDICATED IN CASE PLAN. 5.) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PAY A MONTHLY SUPERVISION FEE OF $20.00. 6.) DEFENDANT IS ELIGIBLE FOR EARLY TERMINATION REQUEST WHEN ALL CONDITIONS HAVE BEEN MET. 7.) RANDOM DRUG TESTING. 8.) CONDITIONS AND TERMS OF PROBATION ARE SUBJECT TO MODIFICATION BY THE PROBATION OFFICER AND APPROVAL OF THE COURT. VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS MAY RESULT IN MORE RESTRICTIVE SANCTIONS AS APPROVED BY LAW. THE DEFENDANT IS REFERRED TO THE COUNTY PROBATION DEPARTMENT FOR A POST-SENTENCE INVESTIGATION AND REPORT. AS A RESULT OF THE CONVICTION(S) IN THIS CASE AND THE IMPOSITION OF A PRISON SENTENCE, AND PURSUANT TO R.C. 2967.28(F)(4)(C), THE DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBJECT TO A PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL OF: UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. THE ADULT PAROLE AUTHORITY WILL ADMINISTER THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL PURSUANT TO R.C.2967.28, AND THE DEFENDANT HAS BEEN ADVISED THAT IF THE DEFENDANT VIOLATES POST-RELEASE CONTROL, THE PAROLE BOARD MAY IMPOSE A PRISON TERM AS PART OF THE SENTENCE OF UP TO HALF OF THE STATED PRISON TERM OR STATED MINIMUM TERM ORIGINALLY IMPOSED UPON THE DEFENDANT IN NINE-MONTH INCREMENTS. IF WHILE ON POST-RELEASE CONTROL THE DEFENDANT IS CONVICTED OF A NEW FELONY, THE SENTENCING COURT WILL HAVE AUTHORITY TO TERMINATE THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL AND ORDER A CONSECUTIVE PRISON TERM OF UP TO THE GREATER OF TWELVE MONTHS OR THE REMAINING PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL. DEFENDANT DECLARED INDIGENT. COSTS WAIVED SUPERVISION FEE WAIVED FINE(S) WAIVED. RESTITUTION ORDERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000.00 TO NICOLAS GILLENTINE; PAYABLE THROUGH THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT. DEFENDANT TO BEGIN MAKING RESTITUTION PAYMENTS MONTHLY ON 8/1/25. FIRST MISSED RESTITUTION PAYMENT SHALL BE REPORTED TO THE COURT AND A HEARING SCHEDULED. CASE PLAN SHALL INCLUDE CONTINUATION WITH SOBRIETY PROGRAMMING. DEFENDANT TO RE-ENGAGE WITH MURTIS TAYLOR AND TAKE ALL MEDICATION PRESCRIBED. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. DEFENDANT TO FORFEIT TO CLEVELAND POLICE DEPARTMENT: ALL ITEMS SEIZED AS AGREED. NO CONTACT WITH VICTIM(S) DEFENDANT ADVISED OF POST RELEASE CONTROL. AS A RESULT OF THE CONVICTION(S) IN THIS CASE AND THE IMPOSITION OF A PRISON SENTENCE, AND PURSUANT TO R.C. 2967.28, THE DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBJECT TO A PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL OF: UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. THE ADULT PAROLE AUTHORITY WILL ADMINISTER THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL PURSUANT TO R.C.2967.28, AND THE DEFENDANT HAS BEEN ADVISED THAT IF THE DEFENDANT VIOLATES POST-RELEASE CONTROL, THE PAROLE BOARD MAY IMPOSE A PRISON TERM AS PART OF THE SENTENCE OF UP TO HALF OF THE STATED PRISON TERM OR STATED MINIMUM TERM ORIGINALLY IMPOSED UPON THE DEFENDANT IN NINE-MONTH INCREMENTS. IF WHILE ON POST-RELEASE CONTROL THE DEFENDANT IS CONVICTED OF A NEW FELONY, THE SENTENCING COURT WILL HAVE AUTHORITY TO TERMINATE THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL AND ORDER A CONSECUTIVE PRISON TERM OF UP TO THE GREATER OF TWELVE MONTHS OR THE REMAINING PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL. ORIGINAL BOND CONTINUED. STATE OF OHIO ORDERED TO NOTIFY VICTIM OF DATE & TIME OF SENTENCING. SENTENCING SET FOR 11/12/2025 AT 09:30 AM. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO APPEAR MAY RESULT IN A CAPIAS. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. nightmare_events: 10/16/2025 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. nightmare_events: 10/28/2025 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO HAVE COMPLAINING WITNESS(ES) PRESENT FOR TRIAL DATE AT 9:00 AM. FAILURE OF THE COMPLAINING WITNESS TO APPEAR, WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN, WILL RESULT IN DISMISSAL OF THE CASE FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE. 12/08/2025 CPDL2 12/08/2025 08:20:22

IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. THE STATE IS ORDERED TO SUBPOENA/NOTIFY ANY COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S) TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT AT THE FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR ANY DATE ON WHICH A PLEA HAS BEEN ACCEPTED AND IS SET TO PROCEED. THE COURT HAS THE OPTION OF PROCEEDING DIRECTLY TO SENTENCING AT THE TIME OF PLEA. THE STATE WILL BE REQUIRED TO STATE ON THE RECORD WHAT EFFORTS WERE MADE TO NOTIFY THE COMPLAINING WITNESS, ALLEGED VICTIM(S), AND IF PRESENT, TO NOTIFY THE COURT BEFORE THE PROCEEDING, IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO ALLOW THEM THE OPPORTUNITY TO SPEAK. VICTIM(S) WHO APPEAR AT A PLEA OR FINAL PRETRIAL WHERE A PSI IS ULTIMATELY ORDERED, MAY, IF THEY CHOOSE, PLACE THEIR STATEMENT ON THE RECORD AT THAT TIME AND EITHER APPEAR AGAIN AT SENTENCING, OR WAIVE THEIR PRESENCE AT THE LATER DATE. IN THE EVENT OF A WAIVER AT ANY LATER SET SENTENCING DATE, THE COURT WILL INCORPORATE THE REMARKS INTO THE RECORD OF SENTENCING. ANY REQUEST OF THE STATE TO BE RELIEVED OF COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER MUST BE MADE BY WRITTEN MOTION, FILED NO LESS THAN 5 DAYS BEFORE THE SCHEDULED FINAL PRETRIAL AND/OR PLEA DATE. THE COURT ORDERS THAT THE MARK ON THE FILE MUST BE EMAILED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL 48 HOURS BEFORE THE FINAL PT. THE COURT IS TO BE NOTIFIED IF NO MARK REQUEST WAS MADE BY THE DEFENDANT. STATE PLACED THE PLEA OFFER AND REQUISITE PENALTIES ON THE RECORD. COUNSEL ADVISED DEFENDANT ACCEPTS THE OFFER AND WAIVES ANY DEFECTS AS IT RELATES TO THE AMENDMENT OF THE INDICTMENT. DEFENDANT FULLY ADVISED IN OPEN COURT OF HIS/HER CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHTS AND PENALTIES. DEFENDANT RETRACTS FORMER PLEA OF NOT GUILTY AND ENTERS A PLEA OF GUILTY TO HAVING WEAPONS WHILE UNDER DISABILITY R.C. 2923.13 A(2) F3 WITH FORFEITURE SPECIFICATION(S) (2941.1417) AS CHARGED IN COUNT(S) 1 OF THE INDICTMENT. COUNT(S) 2 IS/ARE NOLLED. COURT ACCEPTS DEFENDANT'S GUILTY PLEA. DEFENDANT WAIVES PSI. PSI WAIVED WITHOUT OBJECTION. DEFENDANT TO FORFEIT TO CLEVELAND METROPOLITAN HOUSING AUTHORITY: A 9MM BERETTA PX4 STORM; SERIAL #:PZ20841 DEFENDANT ADVISED OF POST RELEASE CONTROL FOR UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. PARTIES AGREED TO PROCEED TO SENTENCING. STATE ADVISED THE COURT OF THE FACTS IN THE CASE AND DEFENDANT'S CRIMINAL HISTORY. DEFENSE COUNSEL AND DEFENDANT ADDRESSED THE COURT. COUNSEL ADVISED DEFENDANT IS RECIEVING MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES AND SUBMITTED DOCUMENTATION FROM CARE ALLIANCE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH.. THE COURT CONSIDERED ALL REQUIRED FACTORS OF THE LAW. THE COURT FINDS THAT A COMMUNITY CONTROL SANCTION WILL ADEQUATELY PROTECT THE PUBLIC AND WILL NOT DEMEAN THE SERIOUSNESS OF THE OFFENSE. IT IS THEREFORE ORDERED THAT THE DEFENDANT IS SENTENCED TO ONE AND ONE HALF YEARS OF COMMUNITY CONTROL ON EACH COUNT, UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF THE ADULT PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITH THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1.) DEFENDANT TO ABIDE BY ALL RULES AND REGULATIONS OF THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT. 2.) DEFENDANT TO BE SUPERVISED BY: GROUP C 3.) REPORT MONTHLY OR AS DIRECTED BY THE PROBATION OFFICER. 4.) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PAY A MONTHLY SUPERVISION FEE OF $20.00. 5.) DEFENDANT IS ELIGIBLE FOR EARLY TERMINATION REQUEST WHEN ALL CONDITIONS OR DOSAGE HOURS HAVE BEEN MET AN WHEN APPLICABLE WITH MARSY'S LAW. 6.) RANDOM DRUG TESTING. 7.) ATTEND PROGRAMMING AS DIRECTED AS INDICATED IN THE CASE PLAN. 8.) CONDITIONS AND TERMS OF PROBATION ARE SUBJECT TO MODIFICATION BY THE PROBATION OFFICER AND APPROVAL OF THE COURT. COMMUNITY CONTROL IS SUBJECT TO EXTENSION. VIOLATION OF THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS MAY RESULT IN EXTENSION OF THE PERIOD OF SUPERVISION, MORE RESTRICTIVE SANCTIONS, OR A PRISON TERM OF 1 YEAR AS APPROVED BY LAW. AS A RESULT OF THE CONVICTION(S) IN THIS CASE AND THE IMPOSITION OF A PRISON SENTENCE, AND PURSUANT TO R.C. 2967.28, THE DEFENDANT MAY BE SUBJECT TO A PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL OF: UP TO 2 YEARS OF PRC AT THE DISCRETION OF THE PAROLE BOARD. THE ADULT PAROLE AUTHORITY WILL ADMINISTER THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL PURSUANT TO R.C.2967.28, AND THE DEFENDANT HAS BEEN ADVISED THAT IF THE DEFENDANT VIOLATES POST-RELEASE CONTROL, THE PAROLE BOARD MAY IMPOSE A PRISON TERM AS PART OF THE SENTENCE OF UP TO HALF OF THE STATED PRISON TERM OR STATED MINIMUM TERM ORIGINALLY IMPOSED UPON THE DEFENDANT IN NINE-MONTH INCREMENTS. IF WHILE ON POST-RELEASE CONTROL THE DEFENDANT IS CONVICTED OF A NEW FELONY, THE SENTENCING COURT WILL HAVE AUTHORITY TO TERMINATE THE POST-RELEASE CONTROL AND ORDER A CONSECUTIVE PRISON TERM OF UP TO THE GREATER OF TWELVE MONTHS OR THE REMAINING PERIOD OF POST-RELEASE CONTROL. DEFENDANT IS PROHIBITED FROM USING ILLICIT DRUGS; FROM CONSUMING ALCOHOL; AND FROM USING RECREATIONAL AND/OR MEDICINAL MARIJUANA. DEFENDANT TO CONTINUE ENGAGING WITH SERVICES AND PROGRAMMING RECIEVED FROM CARE ALLIANCE. COMPLIANCE HEARING IS SET FOR 07/17/2026 AT 10:00 AM. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO APPEAR MAY RESULT IN A CAPIAS. DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE A POST-SENTENCE INVESTIGATIVE REPORT. nightmare_events: 04/08/2026 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET. nightmare_events: 04/08/2026 - ANY ISSUES REGARDING DISCOVERY ARE TO BE BROUGHT TO THE ATTENTION OF THE COURT IMMEDIATELY. ANY PARTY SEEKING AN EXTENSION TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY IS ORDERED TO FILE A MOTION REQUESTING THE EXTENSION AND STATE SPECIFIC REASONS FOR THE DELAY. IT IS ORDERED THAT ANY MATERIALS MARKED "FOR COUNSEL ONLY" ARE FOR THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF COUNSEL, AND NOT TO BE SHARED WITH ANY PERSONS, INCLUDING THE DEFENDANT, EXCEPTING HOWEVER, THAT THE ATTORNEY MAY VERBALLY DISCUSS THE CONTENTS OF SAID DISCOVERY MATERIALS, IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. SHOULD ANY ATTORNEY BELIEVE THAT THE DESIGNATION INTERFERES WITH THE RIGHT TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL, SAID COUNSEL IS TO FILE THE APPROPRIATE MOTION WITH THIS COURT, WHO WILL THEN DETERMINE IF THE DESIGNATION IS APPROPRIATE, OR IF THE MATERIALS THEMSELVES MAY BE SHARED IN ORDER TO PROPERLY PREPARE FOR TRIAL. COUNSEL IS NOTIFIED THAT ANY ALLEGED BREACH OF THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN A HEARING ON CONTEMPT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO PERSON MAY REQUEST A DIVERSION BACKGROUND CHECK, ELIGIBILITY PACKET OR ASSESSMENT FROM THE COURT'S PROBATION DEPARTMENT WITHOUT A PRIOR ORDER OF THIS COURT. THE PROBATION DEPARTMENT IS PROHIBITED FROM ACTING ON ANY REQUEST MADE BY ANYONE WITHOUT THIS COURT'S JOURNALIZED ORDER, AND IS ALSO ORDERED TO REPORT TO THE COURT, ANY REQUESTS MADE FOR THIS INFORMATION WITHOUT A SIGNED ORDER FROM THE COURT. ANY PERSON WHO VIOLATES THIS ORDER IS SUBJECT TO A HEARING ON SANCTIONS, INCLUDING POSSIBLE CONTEMPT. ANY REQUEST FOR CONSIDERATION OF DIVERSION OR ANY SPECIALTY DOCKET OR PROGRAM, MUST BE MADE PRIOR TO CLOSE OF NON-EXPERT DISCOVERY DATE. SHOULD COUNSEL REQUEST AN EXPERT AT TRIAL, THE MOTION MUST CONTAIN AN AFFIRMATIVE STATEMENT THAT THE EXPERT INTENDED FOR HIRE WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THE TRIAL DATE ALREADY SET.
